title: Anchorage Daily News: Alaska's Newspaper
summary: Outdoors hot line SITE OF THE WEEK Published: February 26, 2006 Last Modified: March 5, 2006 at 04:29 AM ARCHERY 3D shoot set for March 5 at Elmendorf's Six-Mile Lake The Black Sheep Archery Club hosts a 3D archery shoot beginning at 10 a.m. March 5 at the Black Sheep Bowmen Archery Range on Six-Mile Lake at Elmendorf Air Force Base. It is open to everyone. The cost is $10 for nonmembers, $8 for members. Registration is at 9 a.m., and competition begins at 10 a.m. Nonmembers should be at the Boniface gate no later than 8:30 a.m. to get on base. For details, call 753-1855 or visit www.blacksheep archery.com . ASTRONOMY Space telescopes are topic of nature center program Learn about space telescopes at 7 p.m. Friday at the Eagle River Nature Center. Scott Girard will lead a 60- to 90-minute …

title: In bear country, you just can't be too careful (7-17-2005)
summary: HULA HULA RIVER -- No one will ever know how Richard and Kathy Huffman came to be killed by a grizzly bear along this river in late June. It is clear the attack by a healthy, 9-year-old male bear was not the fault of the Anchorage couple. They kept a clean camp. They pitched their tent where they could see bears coming and where the bears could see them to avoid confrontation. They stored their food in bear-proof containers. And they carried a gun just in case all of these bear avoidance efforts failed and they had to defend themselves. They were killed by a bear anyway, and authorities said the dead rafters had done everything right. That is true, but there are also things they could have done differently. "If they did everything right, and they died, what's the message we're giving the p…
